mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in housing units sold over the past 3 months signals a strengthening demand for housing, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% decline in industrial production over the past 2 months signals a weakening supply chain, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the unemployment rate over the past 3 months signals a weakening labor market, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in weakening labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in real GDP over the past 2 quarters signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the consumer price index over the past 2 months signals a strengthening monetary policy, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening monetary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% decline in industrial production over the past 2 months signals a weakening supply chain, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in weakening supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in real disposable personal income over the past 2 quarters signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
